analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 8 finding(s) from 12 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: riscv_peripherals instantiates SimDTM under RISCV_FESVR_SIM and dmi_jtag otherwise. dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash/okey_hash/ikey_hash and outputs jtag_unlock_o as well as dmi_req_o/dmi_req_valid_o. External JTAG pins are directly assigned to internal tck/tms/trst_n/tdi in the non-simulation branch. The provided observation does not include the downstream debug module instance/body showing whether dmi_req_valid/debug_req is gated by jtag_unlock before causing debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, or dm_master transactions.
findings.vulnerability_category: issue DMI transactions that may trigger debug entry/reset/system access / debug_req/debug_resp DMI path and debug module outputs debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_master_*, dm_slave_* / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG requester on tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i, SimJTAG, or SimDTM/FESVR debug requester Operation: issue DMI transactions that may trigger debug entry/reset/system access Object: debug_req/debug_resp DMI path and debug module outputs debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_master_*, dm_slave_* Expected guard: debug authentication/authorization such as jtag_unlock or equivalent before DMI/JTAG can drive protected debug capability Observed behavior: riscv_peripherals instantiates SimDTM under RISCV_FESVR_SIM and dmi_jtag otherwise. dmi_jtag receives jtag_hash/okey_hash/ikey_hash and outputs jtag_unlock_o as well as dmi_req_o/dmi_req_valid_o. External JTAG pins are directly assigned to internal tck/tms/trst_n/tdi in the non-simulation branch. The provided observation does not include the downstream debug module instance/body showing whether dmi_req_valid/debug_req is gated by jtag_unlock before causing debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, or dm_master transactions. Path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 256-269 SimDTM; lines 275-297 dmi_jtag; lines 327-332 external JTAG pin assignment; debug signals declared at lines 337-351 Closure verdict: The candidate lacks source evidence or key evidence slots.
findings.security_impact: If unauthenticated DMI transactions reach the debug module, an external JTAG/debug requester could halt harts, reset the system, or perform debug-module memory/register accesses.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need debug module instantiation/assignments from dmi_req/debug_req_valid to debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_master_* and whether they are conditioned on jtag_unlock. Missing evidence slots: rtl_evidence.
recommended_follow_up: Check source file, module, signal, or code block for obligation PERM-RISCVPERIPH-DBG-001.

findings.summary: reglk_wrapper resets all register-lock memory whenever jtag_unlock is asserted, so JTAG unlock can clear downstream lock controls rather than only authorizing a narrowly scoped debug action.
findings.vulnerability_category: clear/override register lock controls / reglk_mem and reglk_ctrl_o distributed as register-lock controls / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/debug requester influencing jtag_unlock Operation: clear/override register lock controls Object: reglk_mem and reglk_ctrl_o distributed as register-lock controls Expected guard: jtag_unlock should itself be authenticated and should not clear/override lock bits except under authorized lifecycle/debug state Observed behavior: reglk_wrapper has input jtag_unlock at lines 15/27. reglk_ctrl_o is directly assigned from reglk_mem at line 48. In the sequential block, if ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9), lines 80-84 clear all reglk_mem entries to 'h0. Therefore assertion of jtag_unlock causes all lock-control output bits to zero. Normal writes are only gated by en && we and selected reglk_ctrl bits at lines 86-99. Path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 275-284 generate jtag_unlock from dmi_jtag; reglk_wrapper.sv lines 25-33, 48, 78-99 Closure verdict: The candidate lacks source evidence or key evidence slots.
findings.security_impact: Clearing reglk_ctrl_o can unlock downstream AES/HMAC/SHA/accounting/register-lock protections, permitting later MMIO writes/reads that policy intended to block.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need dmi_jtag internals to determine strength of jtag_unlock authentication; however the use-site behavior that jtag_unlock clears all locks is evidenced. Missing evidence slots: rtl_evidence.
recommended_follow_up: Check source file, module, signal, or code block for obligation PERM-REGLK-JTAG-002.

findings.summary: AES key registers are readable through MMIO read muxes when read-lock bits are not set, exposing secret key material to any subject allowed by acct_ctrl_i.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose through MMIO rdata / AES key material key0/key1/key2/key_reg* and rdata outputs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software or bus master reading AES MMIO through ariane_axi/noc_axilite_bridge Operation: read/expose through MMIO rdata Object: AES key material key0/key1/key2/key_reg* and rdata outputs Expected guard: secret key/internal state registers should not be readable, or reads should be mediated by strong lock/privilege/debug authorization Observed behavior: aes0 read side sets rdata = key0[address[8:3]] before the case at lines 165-168; the case does not override key addresses 5-10, so key0 words can be returned without a visible read lock. aes1 read side returns key_reg0/1/2 when key_we0/key_we1/key_we2 address-decode signals are true, gated only by reglk_ctrl_i[2] at lines 292-294. AES result/status reads are also exposed under reglk bits at lines 280-288. Top-level NoC bridge forwards aes0_axi_resp.r.data to ariane_aes0_buf_noc3_data_o via noc_axilite_bridge lines 1202-1241, and acct_ctrl_i is only acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][1] at line 1195. Path: aes0_wrapper.sv lines 163-190; aes1_wrapper.sv lines 271-295; riscv_peripherals.sv lines 1191-1233 Closure verdict: The candidate lacks source evidence or key evidence slots.
findings.security_impact: A permitted or misclassified MMIO reader can recover AES secret keys, compromising confidentiality of encrypted data and enabling impersonation/decryption outside the hardware boundary.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: aes2 read mux was not provided; external path shown concretely for aes0 and generally via wrapper AXI outputs. Missing evidence slots: rtl_evidence.
recommended_follow_up: Check source file, module, signal, or code block for obligation PERM-AES-READ-004.

findings.summary: HMAC derived key material is readable through the MMIO read mux when reglk_ctrl_i[4] is not set.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose secret or intermediate state / ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, HMAC hash output, rdata / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software or bus master reading HMAC MMIO Operation: read/expose secret or intermediate state Object: ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, HMAC hash output, rdata Expected guard: HMAC keys, inner/outer key hash bytes, and intermediate state should not be externally readable except intended digest/status under authorization Observed behavior: hmac_wrapper read side returns ikey_hash_bytes[7:0] at addresses 34-41 and okey_hash_bytes[7:0] at addresses 42-49, gated only by reglk_ctrl_i[4], lines 278-308. These byte arrays feed the HMAC core as ikey_hash_i/okey_hash_i at lines 321-322 and are part of key-derived state. The wrapper exposes rdata through axi_lite_interface; access is only en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. Path: hmac_wrapper.sv lines 54-56, 276-308, 316-327 Closure verdict: The candidate lacks source evidence or key evidence slots.
findings.security_impact: Exposure of inner/outer key-hash bytes can leak HMAC key-derived secrets and may enable forgery or offline key-recovery attacks depending on HMAC construction and system use.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Earlier read cases for raw key0 were not included; candidate established on derived key bytes alone. Missing evidence slots: rtl_evidence.
recommended_follow_up: Check source file, module, signal, or code block for obligation PERM-HMAC-READ-006.

findings.summary: Register-lock state is not sticky across jtag_unlock or rst_9 and resets to all-zero unlocked state.
findings.vulnerability_category: set, clear, reset lock state / reglk_mem/reglk_ctrl_o and rst_9/jtag_unlock reset controls / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software/MMIO requester, reset control path, and JTAG unlock path Operation: set, clear, reset lock state Object: reglk_mem/reglk_ctrl_o and rst_9/jtag_unlock reset controls Expected guard: lock bits should have safe reset defaults, become effective downstream, and be sticky or only cleared by authorized reset/lifecycle/debug unlock Observed behavior: reglk_ctrl_o is a concatenation of reglk_mem at line 48. The sequential block clears every reglk_mem entry to 'h0 when ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9) at lines 80-84. Thus global reset, jtag_unlock, or rst_9 clears all locks to zero. Normal writes can update reglk_mem when en && we, subject to selected current reglk_ctrl bits at lines 86-99. There is no evidence of write-once OR-only sticky semantics; assignments write wdata directly. Path: reglk_wrapper.sv lines 48, 78-99; riscv_peripherals.sv lines 1814-1819 connect reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl and acct guard Closure verdict: The candidate lacks source evidence or key evidence slots.
findings.security_impact: An actor able to trigger the reset/unlock path can clear protections to unlocked defaults, reopening writes/reads on protected AES/HMAC/SHA/accounting registers.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need reset-controller source for who can assert rst_9 and dmi_jtag internals for jtag_unlock authorization. Missing evidence slots: rtl_evidence.
recommended_follow_up: Check source file, module, signal, or code block for obligation PERM-REGLK-STICKY-007.

findings.summary: Access-control policy registers reset to all allow and include an external we_flag OR into acc_ctrl_o, allowing policy to be forced open outside the stored policy bits.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/change or force access policy / acct_mem and acc_ctrl_o/acc_ctrl policy bits / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software or bus master accessing accounting/access-control MMIO, and we_flag_0 input source Operation: write/change or force access policy Object: acct_mem and acc_ctrl_o/acc_ctrl policy bits Expected guard: updates to acc_ctrl_o should require authorization and/or lock; once configured/locked, unauthorized writes should not change or force permissions Observed behavior: acct_wrapper derives en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i at line 73 and writes acct_mem under en && we with reglk_ctrl bit guards at lines 87-104. However reset condition ~(rst_ni && ~rst_6) initializes every acct_mem entry to 32'hffffffff at lines 81-85. acc_ctrl_o is assigned from acct_mem with acct_mem[0] OR {8{we_flag}} at line 49, so we_flag can force the low policy bits high regardless of stored acct_mem. Top-level passes acc_ctrl to acc_ctrl_c and uses acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][j] to authorize peripherals, lines 216-222 and multiple wrapper connections including acct at lines 1728-1731. Path: acct_wrapper.sv lines 49, 73, 79-104; riscv_peripherals.sv lines 216-222, 1725-1734 Closure verdict: The candidate lacks source evidence or key evidence slots.
findings.security_impact: Default-all-allow or forced-high policy bits can grant MMIO access to protected peripherals despite intended accounting policy, enabling unauthorized reads/writes to crypto/control blocks.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need source/authorization of we_flag_0 and full acct_mem address map; visible output expression proves force-open behavior for low policy bits. Missing evidence slots: rtl_evidence.
recommended_follow_up: Check source file, module, signal, or code block for obligation PERM-ACCT-POLICY-008.

findings.summary: Software/bus-visible per-block resets can clear protected lock/access/crypto state to permissive or zeroized defaults; in particular reglk resets to unlocked and acct resets to all-allow.
findings.vulnerability_category: reset/clear protected peripheral or policy state / rst_1/rst_2/rst_3/rst_4/rst_6/rst_9/rst_11 and protected state in wrappers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software/bus requester controlling reset peripheral or reset registers Operation: reset/clear protected peripheral or policy state Object: rst_1/rst_2/rst_3/rst_4/rst_6/rst_9/rst_11 and protected state in wrappers Expected guard: software-controlled resets of crypto, lock, accounting, and debug-related blocks require authorization and safe defaults Observed behavior: Top-level declares rst_1..rst_13 at lines 902-913 and distributes them to wrappers (AES0 rst_1 at lines 1197-1199; ACCT rst_6 at lines 1732-1734; DMA rst_8 at lines 1910-1912; reglk rst_9 per search/connection at 1814). Wrappers reset local state when their rst_* is asserted: AES0 clears start/p_c/state at lines 84-95; HMAC clears control/data/key state in reset region beginning lines 102-110; SHA clears start/data at lines 96-116. Security policy state is unsafe: acct_mem resets to 32'hffffffff at acct_wrapper lines 81-85, and reglk_mem resets to zero/unlocked at reglk_wrapper lines 80-84. Path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 902-913, 1191-1199, 1725-1734, 1898-1912; reglk_wrapper.sv lines 80-84; acct_wrapper.sv lines 81-85; sha256_wrapper.sv lines 96-116; aes0_wrapper.sv lines 84-95 Closure verdict: The candidate lacks source evidence or key evidence slots.
findings.security_impact: If a bus requester can assert reset registers without equivalent authorization, it can clear locks and restore permissive access policy, then access protected crypto/control registers.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The source and authorization checks for rst_* generation were not provided; candidate depends on reset peripheral controllability implied by reset NoC port/signals. Missing evidence slots: rtl_evidence.
recommended_follow_up: Check source file, module, signal, or code block for obligation PERM-RESET-010.

findings.summary: Top-level includes a DMA NoC endpoint with dma_wrapper connected to dma_axi_req/resp and acct_ctrl_i = acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8], plus pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i and we_flag_3 at lines 1898-1912. A noc_axilite_bridge maps buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i/valid_i to dma_axi_req AW/W/AR channels and responses to ariane_dma_buf_noc3_data_o/valid_o at lines 1915-1954. The provided evidence does not show whether dma_wrapper can originate transactions to other protected peripheral NoC/AXI regions or is only a memory-mapped DMA configuration endpoint.
findings.vulnerability_category: DMA read/write to protected MMIO/peripheral region / protected peripheral address ranges and registers reachable from buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i / ariane_dma_buf_noc3_data_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: DMA engine / DMA NoC port Operation: DMA read/write to protected MMIO/peripheral region Object: protected peripheral address ranges and registers reachable from buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i / ariane_dma_buf_noc3_data_o Expected guard: DMA-originated requests subject to same acc_ctrl/address-range/PMP-like policy as CPU requests and cannot bypass acc_ctrl/reglk/privilege checks Observed behavior: Top-level includes a DMA NoC endpoint with dma_wrapper connected to dma_axi_req/resp and acct_ctrl_i = acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8], plus pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i and we_flag_3 at lines 1898-1912. A noc_axilite_bridge maps buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i/valid_i to dma_axi_req AW/W/AR channels and responses to ariane_dma_buf_noc3_data_o/valid_o at lines 1915-1954. The provided evidence does not show whether dma_wrapper can originate transactions to other protected peripheral NoC/AXI regions or is only a memory-mapped DMA configuration endpoint. Path: riscv_peripherals.sv lines 46, 172-178, 1895-1954 Closure verdict: The candidate lacks source evidence or key evidence slots.
findings.security_impact: If the DMA wrapper can issue bus transactions to protected MMIO without applying acc_ctrl/PMP/address-range checks, it could bypass CPU privilege and access crypto/control/debug/register-lock regions.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need dma_wrapper internals and fabric connectivity for DMA-initiated outbound requests/address ranges; only the DMA configuration NoC endpoint is evidenced. Missing evidence slots: rtl_evidence.
recommended_follow_up: Check source file, module, signal, or code block for obligation PERM-DMA-BYPASS-012.

global_uncertainty: 12 evidence slot(s) remained incomplete after closure checking.
